{{Short description|Position within a wave cycle; measures the offset of a wave relative to a reference point}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Phase (waves)
| image = Phase shift.svg
| caption = Two sinusoids with a phase difference <math>\Delta\phi</math>.
| topics = [[Wave]], [[Interference (wave propagation)]], [[Oscillation]]
| symbol = φ
| SI_unit = [[radian]] or [[degree (angle)|degree]]
}}

In [[physics]], the '''phase''' of a [[wave]] or [[oscillation]] specifies the location of a point within the wave cycle at a given time. For a [[sinusoidal]] wave, phase is the argument of the sine (or cosine) function:

:<math>y(t) = A\sin(\omega t + \phi_0)</math>

where:
* <math>\omega t + \phi_0</math> is the total phase
* <math>\phi_0</math> is the initial phase (phase constant)
* <math>A</math> is the [[amplitude]]

== Phase difference ==
The [[phase shift]] (phase difference) <math>\Delta\phi</math> between two waves determines how they [[interference (wave propagation)|interfere]]:
* <math>\Delta\phi = 0, 2\pi, 4\pi, \ldots</math> — constructive interference (in phase)
* <math>\Delta\phi = \pi, 3\pi, \ldots</math> — destructive interference (out of phase)
* Other values — partial interference

== Wave equation ==
For a travelling wave: <math>y(x,t) = A\sin(kx - \omega t + \phi_0)</math>, the phase is <math>kx - \omega t + \phi_0</math>. Points of constant phase travel at the [[phase velocity]]:
:<math>v_p = \frac{\omega}{k}</math>

== Phase in quantum mechanics ==
In [[quantum mechanics]], the overall phase of a [[wave function]] is not physically observable, but [[relative phase]] differences between states give rise to [[quantum interference]] and [[quantum superposition]].

== Applications ==
* [[Interferometry]] — measuring tiny displacements via phase comparison
* [[Phased array]] radar/antenna — beam steering via controlled phase shifts
* [[Phase modulation]] — encoding information in phase changes
* [[Holography]] — recording phase information
* [[AC power]] — [[phase angle]] between voltage and current

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Interference (wave propagation)]]
* [[Oscillation]]
* [[Wave]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Wave mechanics]]
[[Category:Concepts in physics]]
